notes: rule.core.humanizer spans two deterministic LINT.* codes (executed live) and two LLM-judged FM.* codes (prose cases, structural-only in CI). The deterministic cases run through engine/gate/pre-gate-lint.js; the prose cases (executed_in_ci:false) are validated structurally + diffed by a keyed maintainer.

cases.name: negpar
cases.notes: Negated-parallelism tell ("isn't just X, it's Y") — deterministic, SOFT/warn. Alone it does NOT bar the Recommended slot, so the pre-gate verdict is PASS (§14.2).

cases.name: clean
cases.notes: No inflation phrasing, no negated parallelism.

cases.name: hype-voice
cases.notes: LLM-judged hype/significance-inflation VOICE register. Bars Recommended (RD-21).

cases.name: humanizer-tell
cases.notes: LLM-judged residual machine-writing tells (rule-of-three, parrot, signposting, follow-trap).

cases.name: on-voice
cases.notes: Clean control for the FM.* prose cases: grounded operator-conviction, no tells.
